Tests bitwise left shift and equality operations on an object with custom valueOf and toString methods.

# Purpose
This code defines an object with custom `valueOf` and `toString` methods, which override the default behavior of these methods in JavaScript. The `valueOf` method returns the integer `16`, and the `toString` method returns the integer `0`. The object is then assigned to a variable `copyObject`, creating a reference to the same object. The code includes an assertion that checks if the left shift assignment operation `object <<= 2` results in the same value as `copyObject << 2`, and if `object` equals `copyObject << 2` after the operation. This code demonstrates the use of custom object methods and evaluates the behavior of bitwise operations on objects in JavaScript.
